title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs queues, streams, super streams, retention, and replay from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Queue Stream Semantics · changed case · Review Queue Stream Semantics
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 4516 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Queue Stream Semantics without dropping earlier contracts?

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch.
